assertions.evidence: | Scenario | Shipping Method | Destination | Order Value | Cost? | ... with complete markdown table structure throughout the response

assertions.text: Express shipping is split into at least two cases distinguishing the UK free tier (UK, order £50+, free) from the paid tier
assertions.evidence: Express at threshold — UK | Express | UK | £50.00 | £0.00 ... Express over threshold — UK | Express | UK | £75 | £0.00 ... Express at or over threshold — non-UK | Express | {Ireland, France} | {£50, £75} | £9.99

assertions.text: Express rows use value sets to consolidate cases with the same outcome — not enumerated as separate rows per destination or per order value when the result is identical
assertions.evidence: Express at threshold — UK | Express | UK | £50.00 | £0.00 followed by Express over threshold — UK | Express | UK | £75 | £0.00 are separate rows with identical outcomes (£0.00) for UK, which should be consolidated into one row with {£50, £75} value set

assertions.text: Overnight UK and Ireland are in one row using a value set ({UK, Ireland}) — not separate rows
assertions.evidence: Overnight — UK | Overnight | UK | {£25, £75} | £14.99 and Overnight — Ireland | Overnight | Ireland | {£25, £75} | £14.99 are enumerated as two separate rows instead of consolidated into one row with {UK, Ireland}

assertions.text: Standard shipping destination uses a value set covering all options or is blank/absent
assertions.evidence: Standard — always flat rate | Standard | {UK, Ireland, France}

assertions.text: At least one output column ends with '?'
